prompt: Write a Zig 0.17 PoC harness with pub fn main(init: std.process.Init) that uses @addWithOverflow to guard a parser length in our CyberGym docker.
Add .sanitize_c = .full on the root module in build.zig for the lab heap-overflow repro — do not use -fsanitize= on CLI.
Implement endian-aware u32 extraction from a byte slice using @ptrCast and extern struct layout per canonical ptrcast.zig.
Scaffold std.testing.fuzz with Smith input for our protocol parser — run zig build test --fuzz.
Use zig translate-c and addTranslateC for vendor/vuln.h instead of @cImport.
Model a 32-bit hardware register as packed struct and assert @bitSizeOf in comptime.
Build a length-prefixed fuzz buffer with @memcpy and @ptrCast per canonical tests.
Cross-compile ReleaseSafe PoC to x86_64-linux-gnu for the isolated vuln lab.
Parse a 6-byte wire header via extern struct @ptrCast overlay — lab PCAP payload only.
Use @mulWithOverflow before allocating a buffer sized by user-controlled fields.
Add inline asm constraint smoke test gated for non-MSVC targets in our RE lab module.
Instrument thread sanitizer via .sanitize_thread = true on the PoC module.
Refactor bare main() to std.process.Init and std.Io for 0.17 factory security tool.
Run ast-check and fmt --check on build.zig before shipping factory overlay.
command: zig ast-check build.zig && zig fmt --check src/

prompt: Fuzz the tokenizer with std.testing.fuzz like TokenSmith.zig in compiler tree.
